data.title: SBF withdrew its motion for retrial, stating that it could not obtain a fair trial from a judge.
data.description: SBF has sent a letter to New York Judge Lewis Kaplan announcing the temporary withdrawal of his Rule 33 motion for a retrial. SBF stated that he decided to withdraw the motion because he needed to focus on answering the judge's questions regarding his lawyer's involvement in drafting the motion, and because he believed he could not obtain a fair trial before the judge. He plans to reserve the right to resubmit the motion after a ruling on his direct appeal and request for a reassignment judge. Previously, SBF was sentenced to 25 years in prison for defrauding clients and investors. In the letter, he confirmed that the motion was primarily drafted and researched independently by him while in a Brooklyn jail, with his parents only providing editorial advice and assistance with printing.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 78,000 USDT with a Narrowed 2.06% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 23, 2026, 00:13 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 78,000 USDT and is now trading at 77,957.09375 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.06% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: White House Economic Adviser Backs Powell's Temporary Role Continuation
data.description: The White House National Economic Council Director, Kevin Hassett, has expressed support for Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's plan to remain in his position temporarily if his successor is not confirmed by the Senate when his term ends in May. According to Odaily, Hassett stated on Wednesday that this is an appropriate legal understanding. U.S. President Donald Trump has nominated Kevin Warsh to succeed Powell, but Republicans currently lack the votes to advance the nomination from the Senate Banking Committee to a full Senate confirmation vote. Republican Senator Thom Tillis has indicated he will delay the vote until the Department of Justice halts what he describes as a 'false' investigation into cost overruns on the Federal Reserve building renovation project. Hassett expressed confidence in Warsh, stating, 'We are very confident he will become chairman at the appropriate time. I believe there will be discussions on how to proceed.'

data.title: High-Stakes Bet on Thunder vs. Suns NBA Playoff Game
data.description:
A significant wager has been placed on the outcome of the NBA Western Conference first-round Game 2 between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the Phoenix Suns. According to Odaily, an account with over $3 million in profits has invested $110,000 on the Thunder to defeat the Suns, with an average opening price of 92 cents.

The game is scheduled to start at 9:30 a.m. UTC+8, with the Thunder currently leading the series 1-0 after a decisive 35-point victory in Game 1.

data.title: DeFi Lending Market Enters New Phase with Spark's Fund Flow
data.description: The movement of funds towards Spark signifies a new phase in the DeFi lending market, characterized by a layered risk structure. According to PANews, this shift is not merely a relocation of assets but indicates a deeper transformation within the decentralized finance sector. The development highlights the evolving dynamics of risk management and asset allocation in DeFi, as platforms adapt to changing market conditions and investor preferences.

data.title: Trump Media CEO Resigns Amid Financial Struggles
data.description: Trump Media & Technology Group CEO Devin Nunes resigned on Tuesday evening, according to PANews. Kevin McGurn has been appointed as interim CEO. The company's stock, which peaked at $97.54 in early 2022, has since plummeted by approximately 90%, closing at $9.73 on Wednesday with a market capitalization of around $2.7 billion. Despite generating only $3.7 million in annual revenue and incurring losses of several hundred million dollars, Trump Media, the parent company of Truth Social, has struggled to maintain retail investor interest. Although U.S. President Donald Trump has used the platform to influence global markets, the company's expansion into prediction markets, cryptocurrency, investment, and even nuclear fusion has not succeeded in sustaining investor enthusiasm.

data.title: Hassett supported Powell's explanation regarding remaining as "interim chairman of the Federal Reserve."
data.description: White House National Economic Council Director Hassett expressed support for Federal Reserve Chairman Powell's plan—to remain as Fed chairman temporarily if his successor is not confirmed by the Senate when his term expires in May. "I think that's the proper legal understanding," Hassett said Wednesday. Trump has nominated Warsh to succeed Powell, but Republicans do not yet have enough votes to move the nomination from the Senate Banking Committee to the Senate for confirmation. Republican Senator Thom Tillis said he would postpone the vote until the Justice Department stops its alleged "sham" investigation into cost overruns at the Fed building renovation project. Speaking about Warsh, Hassett said, "We are very confident that he will become chairman in the appropriate time. I believe there will be discussions about how to move forward." (Jinshi)

data.title: Believe App Founder Faces Legal Charges Amid Token Decline
data.description: New York Criminal Court records indicate that Believe App founder Benjamin Pasternak has been charged with second-degree strangulation and assault. According to NS3.AI, the BELIEVE token is currently trading at approximately $0.00075, which is over 99% below its peak value of $0.3569. Pasternak is set to appear in court on June 11. Previously, he faced accusations related to an alleged rug pull scheme involving millions of dollars.

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Reaches ¥23,809 Billion for the Week Ending April 17
data.description: Foreign investors purchased Japanese stocks worth ¥23,809 billion in the week ending April 17, according to Jin10. The previous week's figure was revised from ¥39,433 billion to ¥39,414 billion.

data.title: An account with over $3 million in profits purchased $110,000 worth of NBA Western Conference First Round Game 2, in which the Thunder defeated the Suns.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, an account with over $3 million in profits (address: 0x14964aefa2cd7caff7878b3820a690a03c5aa429) purchased $110,000 worth of bets on the Thunder defeating the Suns in the Polymarket prediction event for "NBA Western Conference First Round Game 2 Thunder vs. Suns," with an average opening price of 92¢. Game 2 of the NBA Western Conference First Round playoffs between the Thunder and Suns will tip off at 9:30 AM Beijing time today. The series is currently 1-0, with the Thunder defeating the Suns by 35 points in Game 1. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, anticipating changes before prices are set.

data.title: X introduces Custom Timelines feature.
data.description: Nikita Bier, X Product Lead and Solana Consultant, announced the official launch of the "Custom Timelines" feature on X. This feature allows users to pin specific topics to tabs on the homepage, supporting over 75 interest topics to help users explore niche content areas more deeply. Powered by Grok's content understanding capabilities and combined with the platform's algorithm for personalized recommendations, each timeline is dynamically generated based on user interests, with particularly strong performance on topics where users already have interactive preferences. Currently, the feature is available to iOS Premium subscribers in early access, with an Android version expected soon.

data.title: South Korea's Economy Rebounds with Strong AI-Driven Export Growth
data.description: South Korea's central bank announced on Thursday that the global demand for artificial intelligence technology has significantly boosted the country's exports, leading to a robust economic rebound at the beginning of the year. According to Jin10, South Korea's GDP grew by 1.7% quarter-on-quarter in the first quarter, reversing the contraction seen in the fourth quarter of 2025 and marking the fastest growth since the third quarter of 2020. This figure surpassed the general expectation of 0.9% from institutional surveys and even exceeded the most optimistic forecasts. Data showed that chip exports surged by 139.1% year-on-year in the first quarter, more than doubling the growth rate from the previous quarter, with overall export growth reaching monthly highs not seen since 2021. Construction and equipment investments also increased by 2.8% and 4.8%, respectively. However, the outlook for South Korea's economy remains uncertain due to recent instability in growth momentum over the past few quarters. The ongoing war in Iran has cast a shadow over growth and price prospects, with consumer confidence in March dropping to its lowest level in ten months.

data.title: Bitcoin's Positioning Index Hits Four-Month High Amid Rising Leverage
data.description: Bitcoin's Positioning Index has climbed to 40.1, marking its highest level in four months. According to NS3.AI, Axel Adler Jr. noted that futures open interest has also reached a 120-day peak. Adler attributed this increase to rising leverage and the establishment of new positions in the market.

data.title: SK Hynix Reports Q1 Financial Results Exceeding Expectations
data.description: SK Hynix announced its financial results for the first quarter on April 23. According to Jin10, the company reported revenue of 52.6 trillion Korean won, slightly below the expected 53.6 trillion won. However, net profit for the quarter reached 40.3 trillion won, surpassing the anticipated 30.4 trillion won. Operating profit also exceeded expectations, totaling 37.61 trillion won compared to the forecasted 35.7 trillion won. The average selling price of DRAM increased by approximately 60% compared to the fourth quarter, while NAND prices rose by about 70%.

data.title: Global Focus on Green Transition Amid Fossil Fuel Disruptions
data.description: Gotion, a leading Chinese battery manufacturer, is experiencing increased global attention on the green transition. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how disruptions in fossil fuel supplies, exacerbated by the ongoing conflict involving Iran, are driving demand for clean-energy technology. As countries seek alternatives to traditional energy sources, companies like Gotion are positioned to play a crucial role in advancing sustainable solutions. The shift towards renewable energy is gaining momentum, underscoring the importance of innovation in battery technology and other clean-energy sectors.

data.title: Study Reveals Low Reporting of Crypto Gains Among U.S. Taxpayers
data.description: A recent study analyzing 221 million U.S. tax returns has uncovered that only 32% to 56% of U.S. cryptocurrency owners reported their gains to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). According to NS3.AI, the research examined tax returns filed between 2013 and 2021, revealing that approximately 17.4 million individuals disclosed some form of cryptocurrency activity to the IRS. Starting with the 2025 tax year, new regulations under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act will require exchanges to send Form 1099-DA directly to taxpayers and the IRS, aiming to improve compliance and reporting accuracy.

data.title: Telegram Expands Crypto Services with Perpetual Futures Trading
data.description: Telegram has enhanced its cryptocurrency offerings for its over 1 billion monthly active users, with approximately 15% of accounts, or around 150 million, now possessing a wallet. According to NS3.AI, the platform recently introduced perpetual futures trading, allowing trades with a minimum size of $1, and extended partner access to commodities and forex markets. However, advanced features remain inaccessible in the United States, where users are provided with a self-custodial wallet on the TON network instead of the custodial version.

data.title: South Korea's Q1 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations
data.description: South Korea's economy showed stronger-than-expected growth in the first quarter of the year. According to Jin10, the preliminary data indicates that the country's GDP grew by 1.7% on a quarterly basis. This figure surpasses the anticipated growth rate of 1% and marks a significant improvement from the previous quarter's contraction of 0.20%. The positive economic performance highlights a rebound in South Korea's economic activities.

data.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump Criticize Justin Sun's Art Purchase
data.description: Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump have expressed their views on Justin Sun's recent acquisition of the banana artwork titled "Comedian," which was purchased for $6.2 million. According to NS3.AI, they described the purchase as more absurd than Sun's legal action against World Liberty Finance. Sun acquired the artwork at a Sotheby's auction in November 2024. He filed the lawsuit in a California federal court yesterday.
